title: Push and Pop
description: Moving between screens with Navigator methods.

slides.title: Push and Pop 🔁
slides.content:
# Basic Navigation

* `Navigator.push()` – adds a new route to the stack.
* `Navigator.pop()` – removes the current route, returning to the previous one.

You typically use `MaterialPageRoute` to create a route with platform-appropriate transitions.

quizData.question: What happens when you call Navigator.pop(context)?

options.text: The current route is removed and you go back

quizData.explanation: pop removes the topmost route from the stack, revealing the previous screen.

slides.title: Push and Pop Example
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Pop with result

You can pass data back when popping: `Navigator.pop(context, result);`
